writeup: The Buffalo Bills kicked off Day 2 of their Mandatory Minicamp at the Kaleida Health Performance Center, generating significant buzz around several key players. Running back James Cook turned heads by reaching a notable milestone, signaling his continued development as a cornerstone of the Bills' offense. Wide receiver Joshua Palmer spoke openly about what's driving him heading into the 2026 season, adding a compelling storyline to an already intriguing receiving corps. Meanwhile, safety Cole Bishop's recovery remained a point of focus, with updates offering cautious optimism about his availability moving forward. All told, Day 2 painted a picture of a Bills squad locked in and hungry heading toward training camp.

articles.title: James Cook's Milestone, Joshua Palmer's Motivation & Cole Bishop's Recovery — Recapping Day 2 of Bills Minicamp
